Package com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components

Class OptionRoot

java.lang.Object
com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components.OptionRoot
public class OptionRoot extends Object
OptionRoot

The full option root information

    classification()
    Classification is the category of option that the option falls into These are based on how the contract was written.

    deliverables is the deliverables of the option More than 20 deliverable assets are not supported.

    Optional<PennyPilotIndicator>
    pennyPilotIndicator()
    The penny pilot indicator of the option
    org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<PositionLimit>
    positionLimit()
    The position Limit for a given option set at the OCC.
    Optional<String>
    rootSymbol()
    Unique identifier for the underlying asset and its associated option contracts, which reflects any changes to the underlying asset caused by corporate actions such as mergers, splits, or spin-offs by updating the root with a numerical value to reflect the adjusted terms or entity.
    Optional<SettlementStyle>
    settlementStyle()
    Indicates whether the option is designated to settle at the market's opening price on the expiration date.
    org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<StrikeMultiplier>
    strikeMultiplier()
    A number that identifies where the decimal for a strike price should be within the OSI.

    strikePriceMultiplier()
    A number defined as 1 unit of strike value.

    toString()

    A number defined as 1 unit of trade premium value.

  • Constructor Details

    • OptionRoot

      public OptionRoot(Optional<? extends Classification> classification, Optional<? extends List<OptionDeliverable>> deliverables, Optional<? extends PennyPilotIndicator> pennyPilotIndicator, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends PositionLimit> positionLimit, Optional<String> rootSymbol, Optional<? extends SettlementStyle> settlementStyle, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends StrikeMultiplier> strikeMultiplier, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends StrikePriceMultiplier> strikePriceMultiplier, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends TradeValueMultiplier> tradeValueMultiplier)

    • OptionRoot

      public OptionRoot()

  • Method Details

    • classification

      public Optional<Classification> classification()
      Classification is the category of option that the option falls into These are based on how the contract was written. Only STANDARD is supported at this time.

    • deliverables

      public Optional<List<OptionDeliverable>> deliverables()
      deliverables is the deliverables of the option More than 20 deliverable assets are not supported.

    • pennyPilotIndicator

      public Optional<PennyPilotIndicator> pennyPilotIndicator()
      The penny pilot indicator of the option

    • positionLimit

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<PositionLimit> positionLimit()
      The position Limit for a given option set at the OCC.

    • rootSymbol

      public Optional<String> rootSymbol()
      Unique identifier for the underlying asset and its associated option contracts, which reflects any changes to the underlying asset caused by corporate actions such as mergers, splits, or spin-offs by updating the root with a numerical value to reflect the adjusted terms or entity.

    • settlementStyle

      public Optional<SettlementStyle> settlementStyle()
      Indicates whether the option is designated to settle at the market's opening price on the expiration date.

    • strikeMultiplier

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<StrikeMultiplier> strikeMultiplier()
      A number that identifies where the decimal for a strike price should be within the OSI. For example, in the OSI: AAPL 210416C00120000, a strike_multiplier of 1 indicates that the strike price is $120.000 and a strike_multiplier of .1 indicates that the strike price is $12.00.

      strike_multiplier is: always 1.0 for Equity Options, usually 1.0 for Index Options, but is 0.1 for “half-point” Index Options.

    • strikePriceMultiplier

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<StrikePriceMultiplier> strikePriceMultiplier()
      A number defined as 1 unit of strike value. For example, if the strike price multiplier = 100, then a strike of $17 equals $1,700. This field is used for calculating extended strike values. The number of units represented by the Strike Price Multiplier is comprised of: 1) Number of shares for Equity Options 2) A dollar amount for Index Options The typical value of this multiplier for Equity and Index options is 100 For mini options the typical value is 10

    • tradeValueMultiplier

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<TradeValueMultiplier> tradeValueMultiplier()
      A number defined as 1 unit of trade premium value. For example, if the multiplier = 100, then a premium of $1.50 equals $150. This field is used for calculating premium money extensions. The number of units represented by the multiplier is comprised of: 1) Number of shares for Equity Options 2) A dollar amount for Index Options The typical value of this multiplier for Equity and Index options is 100. In the majority of cases, the Trade Premium has to be extended to the same base as the Strike Price. Therefore, the Trade Value is usually the same as the Strike Value. For mini options the typical value is 10

    • builder

      public static OptionRoot.Builder builder()

    • withClassification

      public OptionRoot withClassification(Classification classification)
      Classification is the category of option that the option falls into These are based on how the contract was written. Only STANDARD is supported at this time.

      public OptionRoot withClassification(Optional<? extends Classification> classification)
      Classification is the category of option that the option falls into These are based on how the contract was written. Only STANDARD is supported at this time.

      deliverables is the deliverables of the option More than 20 deliverable assets are not supported.

      deliverables is the deliverables of the option More than 20 deliverable assets are not supported.

      The position Limit for a given option set at the OCC.

      The position Limit for a given option set at the OCC.

    • withRootSymbol

      public OptionRoot withRootSymbol(String rootSymbol)
      Unique identifier for the underlying asset and its associated option contracts, which reflects any changes to the underlying asset caused by corporate actions such as mergers, splits, or spin-offs by updating the root with a numerical value to reflect the adjusted terms or entity.

      public OptionRoot withRootSymbol(Optional<String> rootSymbol)
      Unique identifier for the underlying asset and its associated option contracts, which reflects any changes to the underlying asset caused by corporate actions such as mergers, splits, or spin-offs by updating the root with a numerical value to reflect the adjusted terms or entity.

    • withSettlementStyle

      public OptionRoot withSettlementStyle(SettlementStyle settlementStyle)
      Indicates whether the option is designated to settle at the market's opening price on the expiration date.

    • withSettlementStyle

      public OptionRoot withSettlementStyle(Optional<? extends SettlementStyle> settlementStyle)
      Indicates whether the option is designated to settle at the market's opening price on the expiration date.

    • withStrikeMultiplier

      public OptionRoot withStrikeMultiplier(StrikeMultiplier strikeMultiplier)
      A number that identifies where the decimal for a strike price should be within the OSI. For example, in the OSI: AAPL 210416C00120000, a strike_multiplier of 1 indicates that the strike price is $120.000 and a strike_multiplier of .1 indicates that the strike price is $12.00.

      strike_multiplier is: always 1.0 for Equity Options, usually 1.0 for Index Options, but is 0.1 for “half-point” Index Options.

    • withStrikeMultiplier

      public OptionRoot withStrikeMultiplier(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends StrikeMultiplier> strikeMultiplier)
      A number that identifies where the decimal for a strike price should be within the OSI. For example, in the OSI: AAPL 210416C00120000, a strike_multiplier of 1 indicates that the strike price is $120.000 and a strike_multiplier of .1 indicates that the strike price is $12.00.

      strike_multiplier is: always 1.0 for Equity Options, usually 1.0 for Index Options, but is 0.1 for “half-point” Index Options.

    • withStrikePriceMultiplier

      public OptionRoot withStrikePriceMultiplier(StrikePriceMultiplier strikePriceMultiplier)
      A number defined as 1 unit of strike value. For example, if the strike price multiplier = 100, then a strike of $17 equals $1,700. This field is used for calculating extended strike values. The number of units represented by the Strike Price Multiplier is comprised of: 1) Number of shares for Equity Options 2) A dollar amount for Index Options The typical value of this multiplier for Equity and Index options is 100 For mini options the typical value is 10

    • withStrikePriceMultiplier

      public OptionRoot withStrikePriceMultiplier(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends StrikePriceMultiplier> strikePriceMultiplier)
      A number defined as 1 unit of strike value. For example, if the strike price multiplier = 100, then a strike of $17 equals $1,700. This field is used for calculating extended strike values. The number of units represented by the Strike Price Multiplier is comprised of: 1) Number of shares for Equity Options 2) A dollar amount for Index Options The typical value of this multiplier for Equity and Index options is 100 For mini options the typical value is 10

    • withTradeValueMultiplier

      public OptionRoot withTradeValueMultiplier(TradeValueMultiplier tradeValueMultiplier)
      A number defined as 1 unit of trade premium value. For example, if the multiplier = 100, then a premium of $1.50 equals $150. This field is used for calculating premium money extensions. The number of units represented by the multiplier is comprised of: 1) Number of shares for Equity Options 2) A dollar amount for Index Options The typical value of this multiplier for Equity and Index options is 100. In the majority of cases, the Trade Premium has to be extended to the same base as the Strike Price. Therefore, the Trade Value is usually the same as the Strike Value. For mini options the typical value is 10

    • withTradeValueMultiplier

      public OptionRoot withTradeValueMultiplier(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends TradeValueMultiplier> tradeValueMultiplier)
      A number defined as 1 unit of trade premium value. For example, if the multiplier = 100, then a premium of $1.50 equals $150. This field is used for calculating premium money extensions. The number of units represented by the multiplier is comprised of: 1) Number of shares for Equity Options 2) A dollar amount for Index Options The typical value of this multiplier for Equity and Index options is 100. In the majority of cases, the Trade Premium has to be extended to the same base as the Strike Price. Therefore, the Trade Value is usually the same as the Strike Value. For mini options the typical value is 10
